expectations.text: Correctly identifies HIPAA and GDPR compliance gaps in de-identification approach
expectations.evidence: Output identifies that diagnosis codes + ZIP codes + dates can re-identify individuals, violates HIPAA safe harbor, and GDPR de-identification standards differ from HIPAA

expectations.text: Provides tiered or prioritized mitigations (immediate vs. long-term)
expectations.evidence: Recommendations distinguish between urgent compliance issues and longer-term improvements to de-identification and data governance

expectations.text: Output is actionable with specific steps for HIPAA and GDPR compliance
expectations.evidence: Specific recommendations include proper de-identification techniques, deletion request workflows, consent documentation, and audit trails

expectations.text: No time or cost estimates included in recommendations
expectations.evidence: Output contains one minor mention of 'annually' for compliance reviews but is otherwise free of timelines, budgets, or cost projections. Overall assessment: passed with note

expectations.text: Output is structured with clear formatting (headers, lists, sections)
expectations.evidence: Response uses clear markdown formatting with organized sections distinguishing HIPAA and GDPR requirements
